column2: I have returned to the drawer, which has grown familiar to me. I lie on the bed and think.
Amnesia... What past have I forgotten?
I squeeze my eyelids together and search my head for memories, thinking that if I wait...

column1: # 女の子＝クララ、いわゆる潜在意識に縮められる前の記憶が残っている。
# ただ、結局5日目には思い出すことができない。これは5日目の展開を予想外にするためのミスリード

column2: A girl is right by me.
She looks to be the same height as me, or just a little shorter. For some reason, I just know that we're the same age.
A shapeless shadow blocks her face from view. Strangely enough, just feeling her presence is enough to warm my heart.
Suddenly, an autumn wind blows. Fallen leaves glide across the ground.
The sunset above the hill is bright. The waves of the girl's long hair reflect the red light in vibrant, golden sparkles...

Heeey, are you still sleeping?
You better wake up sooooon!
Somebody shouts in my ear and shakes my body violently.
When I open my eyes, I am back in the usual cold drawer, but there is a man I haven't seen before right next to me.
*sigh* Finally up? How can you even sleep in a big place like this? Everyone can see you.
(I feel like I was in the middle of an important dream, but I can't remember any details...)
Hellooo, are you listening? Chio, don't you think this kid's head is too far up in the clouds?
He's probably tired today after going out yesterday. You need to give him a bit of time after he wakes up.
I just got dragged into here. The floor's hard and we have to share the bed--I'm exhausted just trying to get used to it all.
But Mr. Supervisor's been here for ages. He should be used to sleeping side by side on this big mattress.
I'm just saying--you should pop up and get to work already.
I stretch out both arms and get up from the soft bed.
The man has already gone somewhere--maybe he's impatient. Only Chio remains.
Oh, Rin... G-Good morning. Sorry I couldn't give you a wake-up kiss today.
I'm sure you can already tell, but our latest newcomer is pretty pushy.
I wanted to let you get some more rest in, since I know you must've had a hard time during yesterday's outing...
But we already woke you up. Sorry.
...It's fine. Thank you for thinking of me. But why did he wake me up?
Oh, he said he had a request for the supervisor. A very urgent one.
When you're ready, Rin, could you head over to him? I'll let you do whatever you want when you come back.
A man I don't know woke me up in the middle of an important dream. It seems like he has a request for the supervisor. I'll go talk to the new guy, though I don't really want to.
The man has his back slumped and one elbow on the wood-grain wall. He has messy hair and a striking smile, and he is wearing a simple jersey.
Oh, you're finally awake. I thought you were going to sleep forever.
In a bad mood already? Hope you won't be too harsh on me. I have a good reason to be in a hurry.
Just to check--you really are the supervisor, right?
...Yeah, though I've only been here a few days myself.
Hm. You'll help me out then, Supe? I heard you're the one to talk to 'round here if anybody needs a hand.
Maybe you can pull off a few simple magic tricks?
I can't do anything like that. All I can do is give you things that are inside this drawer.
The task of giving someone something has a much heavier meaning here, but I probably shouldn't say that now.
Um... Could I ask for your name? Mine's Rin.
Oh, right. I'm Shimon. What do you think? Sounds cool and intellectual, right?
What's with that face? It's a perfect name for a cool and intellectual guy like me.
(I feel like people who call themselves intellectual actually aren't very...)
What did you need help with? That's why you woke me up, isn't it?
To keep it short, I want something to write with. Pen, keyboard--anything's fine.
Oh... We probably don't have anything like that here. I've been wanting something to write notes on too.

column1: # ワープロは2000年代には消えてて、実際ここで言ってるのはパソコン。
# ただ、この年代だと、シモンのように詳しくない人の中にはパソコンをワープロと呼ぶ人もいた（気がする）

column2: Hmph. Not even a cell phone? I can type as fast as a teenage girl, so I don't need a full-on word processor or anything.
Nope, not even a cell phone.
I made something for Yui before, but it's just two cups and a string, so it can't really type.
I made something for someone named Yui before, but it's just two cups and a string, so it can't really type.
*sigh* You serious, Supervisor? What's even the point of that?
I don't want to cause trouble for you, really. If I were just a regular guy, I'd give up here.
But I'm a special guy, see. Real busy. Lots to do. So my time's just more important than other people's.
So, now that you know that I'm special... Still no pen or keyboard?
Uh, yeah, still none. I'd have handed them over already if I had them.
So, you aren't just keeping them out of the hands of the plebes.
You keep saying you're special, Shimon, but what specifically is so special about you?
As soon as I ask the question, Shimon peers into my eyes. His nostrils flare and his eyes glitter brightly.
Oh, so you want to know? You're curious, eh?
Uh... To be honest, I don't really--
I'm an author. It's an extremely intellectual and respectable profession. Surprised, right?
When you think of an author in your head, I'm sure you envision somebody paler and frailer.
But a guy like me? Who's cool and athletic and smart and creative? If authors like me were just walking around, you'd...
You'd piss your pants, I'm sure.
But why's everyone here such a downer? Just look. I tried talking to that woman there earlier, but she didn't even respond.
You didn't notice her hiding over in the corner this whole time? Look over there.
Shimon points to the right side of the room, where a girl I don't know is staring into nothing, her face blank.

column1: # アカリは変な匂いがするし見た目も少し汚い（冴子の玩具にされていたから）。だけどシモンは女の子に優しいので具体的には言わない。

column2: She gives me the creeps. She's quiet and unfriendly and... I don't know. She just feels off.
But anyway. Since I have nothing to write with, I've got some free time, which is rare for a busy man like me.
Since our schedules are both open...
I'll teach you how to sharpen that mind of yours. What do you think? Want a special lesson from an intellectual and imaginative guy like me?
Normally, you couldn't buy a lesson like this with all the money in the world. You should appreciate each word out of my mouth for its incredible value...
Um, about that... Shimon, could you save that lesson for later?
Th-There's another newcomer, so I have to go say hi! As the supervisor!
Hm? I'll wait then, but just for a bit. I can't say I don't understand. You can't just leave a girl alone.
But make sure to come back when you're done! My lessons are priceless! Hurry before I change my mind!
A listless girl that I don't recognize sits at a slight distance from me. I'm sure it'll be more fun talking to her than Shimon... I'll do my duty as the supervisor and check on her.
Even after I approach the girl, her expression doesn't change. Her empty eyes are looking somewhere else.

column1: # 冴子の体液で濡れている（ゲーム中では明言しない）

column2: Her clothes and hairpin are completely worn out. Her wild long hair is also wet, for some reason.
Just as the silence begins to make me feel antsy, the girl's lips move slightly to utter a short word.
The unknown newcomer uttered one short request... "Water." I have many questions, but for now, I'll go bring the water she asked for.
I take the tool I chose and slowly approach the new girl.
She takes just one look at me, but she does not become angry or sad. For some reason, a faint smile appears on her lips.
...I knew it. You're all worn out too.
You can't even do something this simple...
I look at what I brought. There isn't any of the liquid the girl asked for inside it.
Sorry, I just made a mistake! I'll find something else.
I take the tool I chose and move through the drawer. I hear someone call out from directly behind me.
I turn around and see the girl standing there. For some reason, a faint smile appears on her lips.
...I knew it. You're all worn out too.
You can't even tell people apart...
Sorry, I just made a mistake! I'll be right with you.
The girl takes the cup and stands up tall. Then, just as she is about to pour the water inside on her head--
The girl cocks her head and looks my way, her face still blank. She is still holding the cup high above her head.
Um, if you pour that out here, the walls and floor will get wet! I-I'll find you a better place. Just wait...
The girl tried to take a shower right there after getting the water, but we'll be in for it later if the floor or walls get wet. I'll take her somewhere that's okay to get wet.
I take the person I chose and move through the drawer. I hear someone call out from directly behind me.
I turn around and see the girl standing there. A strangely satisfied smile appears on her lips as she nods.
...I knew it. You're all worn out too.
You can't even tell people apart...
Sorry, I just made a mistake! I'll be right with you.
Th-There! If you're going to sh-shower, do it on top of this!
She takes just one look at me, but she does not become angry or sad. A strangely satisfied smile appears on her lips as she nods.
...I knew it. You're all worn out too.
Taking a shower here will clearly ruin this thing, but you can't even understand that...
Sorry, I just made a mistake! I'll find a better location!
Th-There! If you're going to sh-shower, do it on top of this tissue!
Tissues have a more important purpose. And they aren't great at soaking up water.
...There has to be a better place. Come on--take me there already.
Th-There! If you're going to sh-shower, do it on top of this bed!
A few seconds pass in silence.
Finally, she nods in understanding and moves to the center of the bed. The floor at her feet is slightly thicker and softer.
The girl flips the cup upside-down and pours the water over her head. Her hair and clothes are all soaked...
Wait--you can't get off that while wet! U-Um, should I get you a tissue?
After saying that, she lies down right on the soft floor. She stretches out her arms and rolls back and forth on the bed...
By the time she stands up again, the droplets that had clung to her clothes and hair were mostly gone, so she was just a little damp.
This bed is good for soaking up water...but I'm impressed you realized that when you're new here.
Oh, I forgot to ask. What's your name, if you don't mind?
She opens her eyes silently. She seems surprised when she finally speaks up.
Rin, it's nice to meet you again.
Before I come back to my senses, Akari has already walked out of my eyesight. Right after that, somebody calls out from behind me.
Hey, Rin! Looks like you're done.
I could see you go straight for her, even from here. Bit early to invite her to bed, isn't it?
Wh-What are you saying?! I just took her there so she wouldn't get the floor wet...
No worries, man. Everyone makes mistakes the first time around. And I like that go-getter attitude of yours.
But you can't make mistakes forever. I'll help you out. Real intelligence is a turn-on for women too.
Guess it's time to begin one of my priceless lessons... You ready, my man?
(Why does he keep calling me that?)
Uhhh... Right! I, um, have something else to do.
I talked to Chio and Akari, but there's still somebody I haven't talked to.
I have to say hi, since I'm the supervisor. So let's save that lesson for later, Shimon! Sorry!
You mean Kina, don't you? Since you talked to Chio and Akari, only Kina's left.
You mean Yui, don't you? Since you talked to Chio and Akari, only Yui's left.
I-I do, but you know everyone's names already?
Obviously. If I see a woman, of course I'm going to talk to them.
I'm not trying to pick them up right away or anything. It's just weird saying nothing, so I went around to say hi.
But that Kina woman? Unexpectedly easy. Sure, she's got a prickly attitude, but I can tell she's already fallen for me.
But that Yui woman? Unexpectedly easy. I saw that sweet smile of hers--I can tell she's already fallen for me.
In any case, I'm not the kind of loser who needs to surround himself with women. Go for them if you want, Rin.
You'll understand how great my lessons are all the better if you have more experience under your belt. Go forth and make your mistakes, man!
(I told you--that's not what I'm doing!)
There's still a little person in the drawer I haven't talked to. Shimon says she's fallen for him... Anyway, I'll go see how true that is.
Oh, it's you, Rin. I'm fine. Or I was, until that shitty weirdo of a man started talking to me.
I told him to fuck off since I wasn't interested, but he kept smirking at me.
Guys like that only stop after a punch in the face. Words only work on people who actually know how to use their brains.
*sigh* Sure, somebody dies every night in the drawer, but at least I didn't have creeps trying to hit on me. But even that silver lining's gone now...
Oh, right. Where did you go last night? ...You stink. Like woman's perfume that's too strong.
I went shopping with Saeko yesterday. She wanted me to help her choose an accessory.
Heh, so she put you in her pocket and now you smell like her ass or her boob or something?
...Hmph. That huge woman needs to hear somebody else's advice before picking out some jewelry?
Can't even choose her own appearance without caring about other people's opinions of her... Pathetic.
Looks are a kind of curse. Things are way easier if you can live your life without worrying about how people see you.
Though you can end up like me--caring too little and getting into trouble that way. Wanna hear a fun story?
So, I used to collect old jeans from the US. I played drums a lot in them, so they got worn out after a while.
But I kept wearing them, since I thought it was cool. Didn't even wash them... And after one show, I just left 'em in a locker backstage.
It was an old venue. Didn't even have locks. When I got back, I saw a boy from the other band had opened my locker.
So I took a look. What do you think that kid was doing?
He'd stuck his thing through the hole in my worn-out jeans and was jerking off with it.
I took the jeans back from him, changed right there and went home. Those were my favorite jeans--I felt like if I threw them out, I'd be letting him win.
We ran into each other a few days after... But when he saw me in the same jeans, his face went bright red and he was gone, just like that.
He screwed up his show, and his band broke up some time after that. So yeah, not reacting won that for me. Kina 1, loser boy 0. Gah ha ha!
Just a question, but those pants you're wearing now... Are they...?
My '66 501s! They're the jeans I was wearing then! Man, that's hilarious! Ah ha ha ha!
Don't worry--I've definitely washed them at least once since then. Look. No stains, see?
Anyway, I'm just saying you shouldn't worry about how other people see you! Wear whatever you want!
(Is that really the moral of that story?)
After Kina happily showed me her worn-out jeans, I run away to the corner of the room.
U-Um... Yui, are you doing okay?
Wh-What is it? Um, why are you talking to me all of a sudden?
Oh, um... I was a bit worried, so I wanted to check on you.
To be honest, I'm not really okay. L-Like, yesterday was really surprising...
And then this long-haired guy just came out of nowhere to talk to me this morning...
Oh, but how are you doing, Rin? You disappeared last night.
I was so worried, I couldn't even sleep...
There are dark shadows under Yui's eyes. They look a bit worse than they did yesterday.
Sorry. I just went out to shop with Saeko last night, to help her choose an accessory.
Yui's voice jumps up an octave. Her eyes are sparkling with curiosity.
Oooh, so Saeko and you went shopping! Wow! How nice!
Hey, what kind of fashion does Saeko like? She's ten out of ten both for her face and her style--I bet she'd look good in anything!
Hm, it seemed like she wasn't sure herself. That's why she told me to help her choose, and I think I did the best I could.
Ooooh. Hehe, that sounds so nice! I felt some of my exhaustion go away just listening to you.
Yup! Two young people going out and having fun? Hearing about that is enough to charge anyone's battery back up!
Have you heard of Joint Journey? It's a show where single men and women travel together. After they get close, they declare their love to each other! Isn't that sooo nice?
Oh, sorry! Here I am talking about romance, but you and Saeko aren't that far along yet, right?
I always do this! I just say too much. Relationships like these are always the best right before people realize their feelings for each other...
But as you spend t-time together, i-if you really do think you like her...
You absolutely can't hold back, okay? Do whatever it takes to make her yours. You got it?
O-Okay! I'm holding you to that promise!
A-And if it does work out, you have to tell me everything, including all the steps you take. I-I'll listen to eeeverything...
(Are there more romantics in this drawer than I'd thought?)
After more interrogation about last night's shopping, I finally escape from Yui...
Suddenly, the iPod display fills my vision. The digital clock on the screen lets me known that evening approaches.
I look away from the clock and meet Chio's eyes. I give a quiet nod to accept her wordless message.
(It's almost time for Saeko to return. I'm still not used to this...)
(...but I need to decide who to feed today.)
Time has passed before I know it, and now it is evening. My heart is restless, and I don't think I can get used to this...but I still need to give food to somebody.
I approach Akari with the rolled omelet.
This rolled omelet looks yummy. Would you eat it for me?
Are you sure I should have it?
Er... What do you mean by that?
If that's how it is, it's fine.
Akari brings the rolled omelet to her mouth and fills her cheeks. The sweet aroma of the egg and seasoning waft over to where I am.
As Akari eats silently, her cheeks deflate with every bite. Color steadily comes back to her skin, which had looked unhealthily pale.
The change makes it look like she has just awoken from a long slumber.
I'm glad. You seemed kind of down.
Rin, it seems like you're the one whose spirits are up.
...Or more like you've returned to normal. So much happened, but you're back to how you were after forgetting everything... I'm envious.
...I wonder why Saeko didn't do the same thing to me.
No, I know why. Saeko is testing you.
And it looks like you've cleared the first stage... Congratulations, Rin.
But the second stage... I think you've failed today. You can tell, can't you, Rin? I don't look even the slightest bit delicious.
It's unfortunate that we'll all have to die here. We've been through a lot.
At least I had something nice to eat in the end.
Rin, thank you for the fun memories. Let's have a nice death together.
I'm still flustered, but just as I try to find out the truth behind Akari's words--
I approach Shimon with the rolled omelet.
This rolled omelet looks yummy. Would you eat it for me?
Sorry, I don't like my food to be sweet, though I've eaten desserts for research.
...I can't say. That's connected to the very core of my artistry. Sorry, but just forget what I said.
...So you're not going to eat the rolled omelet? I think Saeko made it.
Okay, let me eat it. I'm sure this will become a very important note for my fieldwork.
After saying that, Shimon puts his hand on the edge of the rolled omelet. The smell of the fluffy egg and its seasoning fill the drawer.
One bite, then another. With each bite, the egg slowly shrinks...and for some reason, Shimon's brows furrow.
It's not as sweet as I thought. Is Saeko actually from Kansai? They prefer salty rolled omelets in the west, right?
I don't know...but I don't think she is.
Hmm. Here I thought all women cooked things with far too much sugar...
But it looks like I'll need to reconsider that belief. Huh...
Now, I'm still eating, but I remember my promise. I said I'd teach you how to sharpen your mind.
Why do you keep making that face? You worried somebody like you can't ever become an intellectual?
No, that's not it... Not exactly...
Heh, then you've got nothing to worry about. Rin, what do you think's the most important thing you need to get smarter?
You really think you can get smarter by studying? You'll just turn into some nerdy four-eyes who's got nothing going for him except his grades.
Anyway. The best way to get smarter...is to understand other people's feelings. That's it.
No matter how much you study, all you can use is your own head. Maybe you'll get better at adding and subtracting, but so what?
On the other hand, if you understand other people's feelings, you can use other people's heads. You can take in how other people think and feel.
Then, you can always look at anything from multiple perspectives. That's the best shortcut to getting smarter.
I didn't think you'd say something like that, Shimon, though I don't know if that's really a way to get smarter...
Really? You need this skill to be an author--it's a career that requires high intelligence. And it helps you talk to women.
Maybe you're still learning...but normally, you can't just invite a woman to bed straight off the bat.
I told you--that's not what I was doing!
Now, now. I can understand why you're embarrassed, but once you get smarter like me, you'll stop making mistakes like that.
B-But do you really think you know how other people feel?
I talked to Kina earlier, but she said she was angry that a weird man hit on her.
Sure she did, but she was actually happily angry, right?
I talked to Yui earlier, but she said she was scared after a strange man talked to her.
Sure she did, but she was actually happily scared, right?
Listen up. Women love men who come on strong. Just think of the first refusal as practice.
Having an arrogant boy fall in love with you, having strong guys fight over you--the rankings are filled with stories like that.
...Hey. Do you mind if I ask something that's been on my mind?
Not at all. What is it?
Shimon, you said you're an author...but do you write cell phone novels?
What are you--?! O-Of course not! Wh-Why would you even think that?
Both Saeko and I have fun reading them every day. If you write cell phone novels, I thought we could have fun talking about it.
Hey, my man. There's something I have to tell you.
It's just as you say. The truth is, I write--
Just as Shimon starts speaking in a serious tone I've never heard from him before--
I approach Kina with the rolled omelet.
K-Kina... Are you hungry, by any chance?
Just... Won't you eat this rolled omelet for me...?
I don't want that creepy woman to eat me! Get that food away from me, got it?!
...Fine. I'll eat that rolled omelet for you. Honestly, it doesn't look half bad.
After Kina mutters that, she brings herself closer to the rolled omelet and begins to eat it in silence.
The aroma of the fluffy egg and its seasoning fill the drawer...
Not bad for my last meal. I had something like this before I ran away from home.
Sausages, cherry tomatoes, two layers of nori seaweed and rice, and rolled omelets. I didn't hate it, but it's some kind of irony having this as my last supper.
...Haaah. What the hell am I doing? I didn't even want to eat this.
Whatever. I feel a bit better after eating. Maybe I'll sing one last song before I go.
I have the deepest voice out of the women here, right? I'm pretty proud of it. Power's everything for metal vocals.
I found some good instrumental stuff in the iPod for once. And I was good at writing lyrics, y'know.
Gonna start now. Clear those earholes and listen up.
♪ Where God dies and thunder stills, humankind will remain.
♪ Where Babel falls and ideas die, the penis will stand tall.
♪ Giant boobs raining down in the apocalypse of Sodom.
♪ Giant boobs raining down in the apocalypse of Gomorrah.
Kina finishes howling her song. Just as I had given a few claps--
I approach Yui with the rolled omelet.
Her eyes snap wide open when she sees what I'm holding.
Um, Yui... I brought you a rolled omelet.
Would you eat it for me?
Hehe, I figured this is how things would go. I mean, I know what happened yesterday...
Okay, I'll eat it. I don't want to trouble you, Rin...
After saying that, Yui puts her mouth on the gigantic rolled omelet. The aroma of the fluffy egg and its seasoning fill the drawer...
...Yum. Rolled omelet's actually one of my best dishes, but this is good too. Did Saeko make this?
I guess nobody else can cook anything this big.
But this rolled omelet is completely different from mine. Do you think Saeko's actually from Kansai?
Right. I'm actually from there, so my parents make their rolled omelets salty...
But I like to put lots of sugar in mine. Make them reeeal sweet.
After all, sweet stuff is a better hit with the boyfriends. Boys like it when girls are obvious like that...
Hey, do you want to talk a little about love and stuff? I can talk and eat at the same time. Or maybe you're not interested?
Hahaha, you don't have to look so nervous! Come on--it'll be better to talk about fun stuff until the end, don't you think?
Like, what kind of people have you dated, Rin? I'm super curious about what the dating scene is like for younger people nowadays.
I have amnesia, so I don't remember anything from before I came here...
I don't really have anything I can share.
Yui stares at me with wide eyes. The corners of her lips are turned down in disappointment.
U-Um, how about you, Yui? If you'd like to talk about it...
Heh heh heh. That's a great question.
Okay, I swear I'm telling the truth--don't be shocked! But since about one month ago...I've been dating an idol!
What's with that reaction? You could be a little shocked at least!
S-Sorry. I don't really know how to react, since I'm not that familiar with idols...
Huh, guess that makes sense. My boyfriend's not at the kind of talent agency that would get him on TV either.

Have you heard of underground idols? They sing and dance like you'd expect, but at smaller places around town instead of, like, huge concert venues. But I like that. Makes the distance between us feel closer somehow.
Hmm... Were you are a fan of the idol before you started dating, Yui?
Hehe, bingo! I was a huge fan since his debut. I went to sooo many of his shows.
Idols usually have a cool and collected image, right? But my boyfriend's got more of an innocent vibe.
He tries so hard both with singing and dancing, but he's not that confident in either... Like, whenever I greeted him up close, he'd break eye contact right away.
He was just too cute. It made me want to see what he was like when he wasn't being an idol...
So, I just took him home with me.
You know how idols have handshake events where you can give gifts to them? Well, I gave him some homemade chocolate.
He's such a good kid. When I asked him to eat the chocolates, he really just ate them then and there.
So after the show, he got sick...and what a coincidence. I was there just at the right time.
I moved him somewhere he could rest and was really gentle with him. I even took some photos for evidence.
A few days after, I told him how I felt...and wouldn't you know it! He said okay right there!
Now, he meets up with me whenever I ask, and he lets me do anything I want.
We actually had plans to meet up this weekend...
So Rin, if you have someone you like, you can't hold back.
Do whatever it takes to express your feelings and seize true happiness. Promise me that...okay?
Yui's trembling hands, pale and unblemished, look completely different to me now. Just as they reach out toward my hands--
I approach Chio with the rolled omelet. Her eyes snap wide open when she sees what I'm holding.
Chio... Would you eat this for me?
If that's your decision, I'll accept it.

Thank you, Rin. I'm sure it was a hard decision...
You've given it your all.
With resignation, Chio brings the rolled omelet closer to her face with both hands.
Then, she nods, as if she has accepted her fate...and begins to take tiny bites out of the edge of the fluffy egg.
...What is it? I'll blush if you keep looking at me like that.
...You made this decision yourself, didn't you?
So you can't look sad about it. I made the decision to accept whatever you chose.
Though I don't really understand your intentions...
But you're going out of your way to raise my Appeal. I'm sure you have your reasons for that, even if I don't know what they are.
...So I'll do what you tell me to. You've given it your all, Rin.
Just as I was wondering how to respond to Chio--
I take another look at what I tried to hand over. An incredibly normal item that wouldn't raise Appeal at all.
(No... This won't make Saeko want to eat anybody.)
(I still don't know what to do, to be honest...but I've given food to somebody both yesterday and the day before.)
(It's too late to go back now. I need to find my resolve and choose who to feed...)

column1: # 2日目と共通になってる。変えるかも？

column2: A low, muffled vibration reverberates from a distance. The sound of heavy footsteps.
There's no mistaking it. Saeko has returned to her room.
Saeko walks through the room with even breaths. Every step she takes shakes the drawer like an earthquake...
Each tremor growing larger and more violent than the last.
Soon, they become too strong for me to stand.
I find myself crouching on the floor.
Next, I hear the creak of something moving.
Then, a tremendous commotion that shakes the whole room...
...as the room we're in--the second drawer of a desk--is pried open by Saeko's fingers.
Hello, cute little people. Were you good today while I was away?
Sorry I'm in such a rush. A little something came up.
But it looks like I have time to change clothes...
I'll have one of you as a snack before I head out.
Saeko's hand comes down into the drawer.
Her unthinkably large fingers fill our vision.
They wander for just a moment, as if carefully making a decision...
And then return to the sky, empty-handed.
Nobody's making my mouth water today.
I suppose you were just an ordinary human after all.
...Whatever. I don't need any of you anymore.
And then, they stop. Right in front of Yui.
S-Saeko... Won't you think this over...?
Her gigantic fingers wrap around Yui's tiny arms and legs...

column1: # 低めの場所でユイが掴まれてる

column2: ...and carry her up into the sky.
Wh-Why are you smiling? Ahaha, this is a bit scary!

column1: # 顔に近づく。ユイがガミガミ噛みつき始める

column2: Um, why aren't you saying anything? Say something already!
Let go of me! Hey! A brat like you's got nothing on me--
Heehee, that was a yummy treat.
I thought she'd be all sweet, but there was a bit of a kick to her.
And she was so full of energy...
And then, they stop. Right in front of Kina.
Finally came here then, giant woman?!
Nothing rock and roll about running! Boil me, bake me--do whatever the hell you want!
Her gigantic fingers wrap around Kina's tiny arms and legs...

column1: # 低めの場所でキナが掴まれてる

column2: ...and carry her up into the sky.
See ya, you giant creep. And Rin.
Neither of you's gonna see the pearly gates. Though neither am I...
Anyway, it's bye-bye time. See ya in hell.
I don't like how she was acting all tough...
But she really fought back when I put her in my mouth. It made her fun to eat.
And then, they stop. Right in front of Shimon.
Uh... Saeko, what are you doing?
Her gigantic fingers wrap around Shimon's tiny arms and legs...
...and carry him up into the sky.
What? Do you want to talk alone?
I'm happy to, of course. I'm great at talking to girls.
W-W-Wait! Wh-What are you planning on doing with me?!
I'll do anything I can, so...
R-Right! Saeko, you read cell phone novels, don't you?!
Ever heard of Cola Float? I'm the writer! So...
Y-You want to know what happens next, right? Please--just let me go for now--aaaAAAHHH--
I can't believe he was writing the novel I'm reading.
Maybe that was a bit of a waste...
But I can't do anything now that he's in my stomach. Anyway...
You were perfect again today, Rin. It looks like you've gotten used to your work by now.
...I'm going out for an errand, so be good and wait for me.
Let's talk again when I return.
